verse_specific_anchors: viṣama-kāla (critical hour, named verbatim in the scenario)
anārya-juṣṭam (unworthy-of-the-noble register that the IC's correction matches)
the senior-to-senior register (Krishna to Arjuna ≠ teacher to novice; mirrored by IC-to-senior-on-call ≠ IC-to-junior)
verdict_reason: The analog is verse-specific. The scenario explicitly quotes the Sanskrit ('kutas tvā kaśmalam idam viṣame samupasthitam'), names viṣama-kāla, and the falsifiability/counter_example explicitly preserve the senior-to-senior register that the verse turns on (Krishna correcting Arjuna, who is the senior warrior, NOT a novice). I considered other random verses where Krishna corrects Arjuna sharply (e.g. 18.66 — 'sarva-dharmān parityajya' — but that is an integrative imperative, not a diagnostic question; or 11.34 — but that is the mid-vision instruction). None has the diagnostic 'whence has this come at the critical hour' structure. This is a tight, verse-specific anchoring. PASS.
